record.title: Bess Beardsley Whitman, 1974-03-01, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.keywords: colleges and universities University of Idaho rattlesnakes families horses stores

point.title: Stories about sister at university
point.synopsis: Her sister Delia Johnson's skilled presentation of her trip from California to the university class. Sister's loss of diary and work when the Administration building burned down' this led to her withdrawing from the univesity

point.title: Stories about mom, rattlesnake, and cougar
point.synopsis: Her recollections of home in California; mother's skill and determination. Mother chopped a rattlesnake who hypnotized the calves. A cougar attack. Mother nursed her and other sick people. They came to Moscow so the girls would get an education, and it was hard to run the farm. Mother boarded a boy in Moscow for nothing.

point.title: Trip on horses from California with family
point.synopsis: Troubles handling horses on trip from California. Their outfit was well prepared for trip. Uncle drank on trip, although he promised he wouldn't. Mother cried when she first saw Moscow. Their uncle who lived here had told them to bring horses because the market was good, but the market collasped and some of the horses died that winter because they didn't have adequate shelter. She had to sell her pony, so she cut off part of his mane. Mother's struggle after she came.

point.title: JC Penney's in Mosocw
point.synopsis: Beginnings of J.C. Penneys Store in Moscow, it had formerly been the Golden Rule. Recollections of Mr. Penney; his gift to them. Their development of Penneys' Stores. Desire of established businesses to keep competition out. Working people liked Penney's.

record.description: Mother's skill and determination. Family's horse drive from California; loss of horses. Development of Penney's Store chain. 3-74 .5 hr RM
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
